Papalia crime family.description: The Papalia crime family is a 'Ndrangheta organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, founded by Antonio Papalia in the 1940s. It is one of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton, with strong connections to the Buffalo crime family. The family was decimated after the 1997 murders of boss Johnny Papalia and his lieutenant Carmen Barillaro, ordered by the rival Musitano family. The last leader, Frank Papalia, died in 2014, but the family is still considered active.

Black Hand.description: The Black Hand was a collection of extortion gangs operating within Italian immigrant communities in early 20th-century American cities. Originating in the early 1900s, the groups sent threatening letters marked with a black hand symbol, demanding money under threat of death or injury. The practice spread from New York to Chicago, San Francisco, and New Orleans, often perpetrated by Sicilian and Neapolitan criminals. Notable leaders included Giuseppe Morello, Ignazio Lupo, Enrico Alfano, and Pellegrino Morano. The Black Hand was eventually targeted by police officer Joe Petrosino.

Barrio 18.description: Transnational criminal organization that began as a street gang in Los Angeles, California, in the 1960s. It is multi-ethnic, primarily Central American and Mexican, and operates in the United States, Central America, Mexico, Canada, Spain, and other countries. Engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, and other crimes, with an estimated 50,000-100,000 members. It has been in decline since the pandemic and the crackdown by Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele.

Lashkar-e-Taiba.description: Lashkar-e-Taiba is a Pakistani Islamist jihadist militant organization founded in 1985-1986 by Hafiz Saeed and others. It seeks to merge Kashmir with Pakistan and destroy India, Hinduism, and Judaism through jihad. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it is responsible for major attacks including the 2008 Mumbai attacks. The group is supported by Pakistan's ISI and operates primarily in Kashmir and globally.

19th of April Movement.description: Colombian urban guerrilla group active from 1974 to 1990, founded after alleged electoral fraud. It demobilized in 1990 and became a political party, later merging into the Independent Democratic Pole.

Los Ciclones.description: Los Ciclones are a criminal organization and one of the five main factions of the Gulf Cartel, based in Matamoros, Mexico. They were created in two phases: one during the time of 'Tony Tormenta' and another in 2015 by 'El Contador'. Their area of influence includes the border crossing between Matamoros and Brownsville, United States. They are engaged in various criminal activities including homicide, drug trafficking, and extortion, and are currently active.

Numbers Gangs.description: South African prison-based criminal organization founded in the late 1800s in KwaZulu-Natal. It operates in most South African prisons through three main factions: the 26s (smuggling), 27s (enforcement), and 28s (protection). Known for its secretive nature, complex rules, and internal hierarchy, it is one of the oldest criminal groups in the world and traditionally does not operate outside prisons.

Organizacion de Narcotraficantes Unidos.description: Puerto Rican criminal organization based in Bayamón, founded in 1995 by José 'Coquito' López Rosario. Involved in distributing heroin, cocaine, marijuana, crack, Xanax, and Percocet, and transporting shipments from Latin America to the United States. Declined after mass arrests in 2009-2010.

Peckham Boys.description: Multi-generational street gang based in Peckham, South London, founded in the 1990s. Known for popularizing road rap music and engaging in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, robbery, and murder. The gang operates in Peckham, Walworth, and Camberwell, and is divided into subsets by age group. Active as of 2025.
